exemplar.title: Using mathematical modeling and calculus to find the optimal substrate concentration for amylase efficiency

detailedExemplarData.title: Using mathematical modeling and calculus to find the optimal substrate concentration for amylase efficiency

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Exceptionally well organized with clear headings, numbered subsections, and a miniâpaper format (abstract, method, results, conclusion).
Logical progression from introduction through modelling, optimisation, validation, evaluation, and extension.
Seamless flow between sections, with aims and linking comments at each part.
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and repeated textbook definitions interrupt conciseness.
Some sections could be trimmed or moved to an appendix for brevity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although conciseness is not uniformly at the highest level, the reportâs structure and logical development are exceptional. The use of clear introductions, linking comments, and a miniâpaper style justify a topâband presentation score.

strengths: Consistent use of appropriate mathematical notation in most places (Vâ, Kâ, mmolÂ·minâ»Â¹).
Effective use of multiple representations: equations, tables, hyperbolic and LineweaverâBurk plots.
Clear linking of mathematical steps to biological interpretation.
weaknesses: A few minor notation inconsistencies (E_7 vs E_T, âvââ vs âVââ).
Some figures have faint labels and one duplicated external image without clear citation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematical communication is clear and consistent overall, with sophisticated integration of representations. Minor lapses do not outweigh the consistent accuracy and clarity, meriting a topâband score.

strengths: Strong personal motivationâchoice of amylase stems from genuine curiosity and biology interest.
Clear narrative explains why optimisation criteria (50â90% Vâââ) were chosen.
Evidence of initiative in exploring three different modelling approaches.
weaknesses: Sections of generic textbook content dilute the personal voice.
Extension on numerical methods lacks a focused case study to deepen personal initiative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows outstanding personal engagement through narrative and choice of topic. Although some generic content tempers the novelty, the clear, consistent personal approach justifies the maximum mark for this criterion.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on strengths and weaknesses of each model, especially the tradeâoffs in doubleâreciprocal plots.
Comments link mathematical findings to enzymatic efficiency and realâworld implications.
Reflection appears at several points, showing ongoing critical thought.
weaknesses: Reflection is meaningful but not deeply critical; biochemical implications could be probed further.
Assumptions (e.g., steadyâstate) are noted but not critically evaluated for realâworld applicability in the conclusion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates clear, meaningful reflection throughout, but does not reach the depth of critical, overarching analysis required for the top band.

strengths: Use of HLâlevel mathematics (differentiation, optimisation, regression, decay modelling) applied correctly.
Derivations and numerical calculations are largely accurate, with only minor typographical and notation errors.
Demonstrates good understanding by interpreting results biologically and validating parameters via two regression methods.
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors and inconsistent notation (E_7 vs E_T, K_max vs K_m).
No quantitative discussion of model fit (e.g., RÂ², residual analysis).
Rigour could be enhanced by extending to more advanced kinetic models.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematics is used at the appropriate IB HL level with high accuracy and clear understanding. The minor errors and lack of deeper rigour keep it in the secondâhighest band rather than the top.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a personal biology interest and deriving the Michaelis-Menten equation independently show significant thinking beyond class work, but the mathematics largely follows standard textbook derivations with limited novel adaptation.

The student explains every step of the derivation, interprets derivatives biologically, and validates parameters via two regression methods, indicating good understanding throughout.  However, rigorous proof or extension to more advanced kinetics is limited, so the strand does not reach the âthorough with rigourâ level.
Derivations and numerical calculations are largely correct; minor slips (k_m expression typo, E_7) are noticed but do not propagate.  Thus accuracy is high with only minor errors.

Most material is relevant to optimising amylase efficiency.  However, the paper is lengthy (22 pages) and repeats textbook definitions (e.g. âlinear functionâ, âquotient ruleâ) that add little to the argument.  Hence it is ârelevant and mostly conciseâ rather than âhighly conciseâ.

Notation for rates, subscripts and units is generally correct (e.g. Vâ, K_m, mmol minâ»Â¹) and calculus symbols are used appropriately.  Minor inconsistencies (K_max once, mis-subscript âE_7â) prevent a fully consistent band 4.

Explanations link mathematics to biology in a mostly clear way; units are usually stated.  A few typographical errors and an occasional unexplained symbol (E_r) reduce full consistency, so band 3 is appropriate.

Differentiation, algebraic optimisation, regression and first-order decay are all at HL course level and applied effectively to a real dataset.  No material is outside the scope (hence not âsophisticatedâ), but the level is fully appropriate and purposeful.

The student employs equations, derivatives, tables of computed values, hyperbolic and double-reciprocal plots.  Graphs generally reinforce the discussion though axes on some images are faint and one figure appears duplicated from the internet.  Overall this is âeffective useâ rather than âsophisticatedâ.

Reflection sections discuss efficiency trade-offs, limitations of Lineweaver-Burk and missing time-series data.  These comments appear at several points, showing meaningful but not deeply critical reflection.

Incorporating three modelling techniques and an algebraic optimisation formula indicates initiative, yet the work stays within conventional enzyme-kinetics methods.  Creativity is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

The investigation follows a clear progression â introduction, modelling, optimisation, validation, evaluation and extension.  Arguments build logically (e.g. derivation of Michaelis-Menten â differentiation â optimal [S]) and the reader can follow the mathematical thread without major leaps, but occasional digressions (very long quotations of definitions, duplicated explanations) interrupt the flow, so the development is clear rather than âexcellentâ.
The student analyses strengths and weaknesses of each model and recognises weighting issues in double-reciprocal plots.  Critique is present but not penetrating enough to merit the top band.

Comments link mathematical findings to enzymatic efficiency (e.g., diminishing returns above 90 % V_max), demonstrating a clear connection to understanding, though the enhancement of conceptual understanding is not strongly evidenced.

Headings, numbered sub-sections and tables/figures give an unmistakable structure (Intro 1.x, Section 2, 3 â¦ 8).  Each part starts with aims and ends with a short comment or link to the next part.  The report reads like a mini-paper with abstract, method, results, conclusion and bibliography; therefore the organisation is exceptional and seamless.

The narrative explains why amylase was chosen and how optimisation criteria were set (50â90 % V_max), demonstrating a personal pathway that guides the exploration.  Nonetheless, large sections are generic textbook content, so the approach is âclearâ rather than âhighly personalâ.

annotations.comment: When describing the methodological challenge, specify which data sets or experimental results will be critically evaluated to clarify the scope of independent thinking.
The observation (c) in Table 5 states Î±â1 yields [S]â0, but it should be Î±â0â[S]â0. Correct this to avoid conceptual error.
The introduction includes rich personal narrative, but some reflections (e.g., early curiosity) could be more concise to maintain relevance and avoid repetition.
The studentâs personal connection to enzyme kinetics is engaging and shows clear motivation, strengthening the explorationâs originality.
The assumptions section lists relevant conditions, but it would be stronger if you justified each assumption with specific enzyme-kinetics reasoning rather than relying solely on citations.
The background paragraph correctly introduces amylase, but placing the Michaelis-Menten equation here (rather than later) would improve logical flow.
Notation for initial rate is inconsistent: equation uses âVââ but later text refers to âvââ. Standardize variable names to maintain clarity.
The inline definition mentions an unexplained symbol âE_râ. Ensure all symbols are defined when first used to avoid confusion.
The textbook definition of a linear function is thorough, but it could be condensed or moved to an appendix to improve conciseness.
Figure 1âs axes labels are faint; ensure that figure annotations are legible and include units on both axes.
Equation (7) on page 6 contains formatting errors (e.g., â\tE + S \tlimsupâ). Check LaTeX or OCR artefacts and correct them for accuracy.
The differential equations for [S], [P], and [ES] are correctly presented and show a thorough use of course-level mathematics.
In the steady-state derivation at equation (5), âE_7â appears instead of âE_Tâ. This typo should be corrected to maintain mathematical accuracy.
Table 1âs headings (â>0â, â<0â, â=0â) are cryptic; clarify in the caption or header that they refer to the sign of d[ES]/dt.
The expression for [ES] still shows âE_7â; correct to âE_Tâ in equation (15) to ensure parameter consistency.
The derivation from equation (13) to (15) is dense; consider inserting intermediate algebraic steps or brief commentary for clarity.
Table 2âs narrative predictions are helpful, but include some numerical examples to show how the hyperbolic curve transitions in practice.
In Table 3, units for Kâ and Vâââ switch between g lâ»Â¹ and mmol minâ»Â¹; ensure consistent unit notation throughout.
The hyperbola definition uses âK_maxâ and âK_minâ instead of âK_mâ. Replace these to avoid notational errors.
Figure 3 appears duplicated from an external source; ensure proper citation and verify that usage complies with IA originality guidelines.
The derivative introduction mentions âdvâ/dISâ; this appears to be a typoâcorrect to dvâ/d[S] to maintain precision.
Consider condensing the formal quotient-rule definition and focusing on its biological interpretation to improve flow.
After applying the quotient rule, the algebraic simplification jumps to the final result. Add intermediate steps to guide the reader through equation (8).
The reflection on the lack of critical points is thoughtful; you might deepen it by exploring biochemical implications of approaching asymptote behavior.
In deriving the optimal [S] formula, discuss the physiological relevance of Î± values (e.g. 0.5 to 0.9) to enzyme efficiency.
Reference to â[S]_{q=0.9}â is unclearâdefine q or maintain use of Î± consistently to avoid notation confusion.
Equation (12) lacks parentheses around the second term; add them to clarify that it is (1/Vâââ) not [S]/(Vâââ[S]).
The least-squares regression section is well-explained, but include discussion of RÂ² or residuals to demonstrate model fit quantitatively.
The integration of the first-order decay equation is clearly presented and shows consistent mathematical communication.
In the conclusion, reflect more critically on how assumptions (e.g., steady state) might affect the real-world applicability of the optimal [S] recommendation.
The extension on numerical methods is broad; consider specifying a case study or enzyme-inhibition scenario to demonstrate personal initiative.
